sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
கல்லென்று உனையார் சொன்னார் கந்தா
கல்லினுள் ஈரம் கேட்டறியாரோ
sections.translit:
kallendRu unaiyAr sonnAr kandhA
kallinuL Iram kEttaRiyArO
sections.en: Lord Muruga, who said that you are a mere stone? Do they not know, have they not learnt from others, that there is moisture even inside a stone?

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
உமை சுதனே இமை காக்கும் கண் போல்
எமை ரக்ஷிக்கும் கருணையுருவே பாலகுருவே
sections.translit:
umai suthanE imai kAkkum kaNN pOl
emai rakshikkum karuNaiyuruvE bAlaguruvE
sections.en: Oh, Uma’s son, the compassionate one, you protect us like the brow that protects the eye. You are the youngest teacher of the universe.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
ஓடி வந்தாய் குன்றினின்று குதித்தவனை
தேடி வந்தாய் தாங்கி அணைத்தாய் தேன்
தமிழ் இசையை நாவில் தந்தாய் உயர்
சிமிழ் மறை முத்தின் ஒளிதனை பரவச் செய்தாய்
sections.translit:
Odi vandhAy kundRinindRu kudhitthavanai
thEdi vandhAy thAngi aNaitthAy thEn
thamizh isaiyai nAvil thandhAy uyar
simizh maRai mutthin oLidhanai paravach cheidhAy
sections.en: You came running. You came looking for the devotee who jumped off the cliff. You caught him at the right moment and hugged him. You inscribed sweet Tamil music on his tongue. You let the radiance of the pearl inside the shell spread everywhere and dazzle. Author’s Note The Charanam of this song references the life story of Saint Arunagirinadhar considered a fabulous saint composer in Tamil literature. Czech Indologist Kamil Zvelbel places the time of the saint to 1370 to 1450 CE. Arunagirinathar’s Thiruppugazh, which he sang upon enlightenment after seeing Lord Muruga, is the crown jewel of medieval Tamil literature. Life story of the saint is detailed in numerous write-ups on Muruga's abode and His acts of compassion. Facts about Arunagirinathar Life story of Arunagirinathar
